commit.message:
Match markdown filename to connected JSON data file

- generate_md.py: accepts data file path as CLI arg; derives .md name
  from the stem (eva_data.json → eva_data.md). Default is data.json.
- serve.py: reads 'filename' from POST /save body; writes to that file
  and passes it to generate_md.py.
- HTML: _fsaWrite() now fire-and-forgets POST /save with h.name so the
  server regenerates the matching .md on every auto-save.
- saveAndDownload(): uses connected filename in toast and download link.
- pre-commit hook: iterates staged JSON files and regenerates each MD.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: guard against null IDENTIFIER tokens from ANTLR4 error recovery

ANTLR4 error recovery can produce declaration nodes with null IDENTIFIER
terminal tokens (e.g. when a function parameter list has a trailing comma
before the closing paren). SemanticAnalyzer now null-guards all such
dereferences to prevent NullReferenceException (CS8785) in the source
generator when processing scripts with commented-out parameters.

Remove Send from Entry/LruEntry, relax Sync bounds (#34)

* Remove Send impl from Entry/LruEntry, relax Sync bounds, bump version to 0.2.1

Entry and LruEntry are RAII lock guards that should not be Send (similar to
MutexGuard). The Sync impl only needs K: Sync + V: Sync since sharing &Entry
only involves shared references, not ownership transfer.

Phase 3B: Implement REST API, CLI args, and placeholder pipelines

- Created etl_core/api/ module with Flask REST API
  - endpoints: GET /api/pipelines, POST /api/execute, GET /api/jobs/<id>, etc.
  - Job queue with async execution and progress tracking
  - Rate limiting and comprehensive error handling

  - Simple interface for remote pipeline execution
  - Job status monitoring and result retrieval
  - Health check and job listing

- Created REST API server module (etl_core/api/server.py)
  - Standalone Flask app runner with configurable host/port
  - Integrated logging and error handling

- Added CLI argument parser (etl_core/cli/args.py)
  - Direct pipeline execution: --pipeline <id>
  - Config file loading: --config <file.json|file.yaml>
  - API server mode: --api --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
  - Pipeline info and listing: --info <id>, --list

- Created placeholder pipeline modules (all COMING SOON)
  - amazon_manager: Multi-tenant Amazon DB manager
  - price_list: Rules-based price list (430+ profiles)
  - catalog_merger: 2-file catalog merger
  - tim_merger: 3-file TIM merger

- Added comprehensive test suites (56 new tests)
  - test_rest_api.py: 30 tests for API endpoints and client
  - test_cli_args.py: 26 tests for argument parsing and direct execution
  - All tests passing (100% pass rate)

- Updated requirements.txt with pyyaml for YAML config support

- Updated etl_core/cli/registry.py with helper functions
  - get_all_pipelines(): Returns all registered pipelines
  - get_pipeline_metadata(): Safe metadata lookup (returns None vs exception)

- 56 new integration tests for Phase 3B (all passing)
- 226 existing tests still passing (backward compatible)
- Total: 282 tests, 100% pass rate

Phase 3B enables:
- Remote pipeline execution via REST API
- Async job management with progress tracking
- Multiple ways to run pipelines (CLI menu, direct args, REST API)
- Foundation for web dashboard and monitoring

instantiates InMemory or SQL-backed repositories based on the
storage_mode config setting. This is the foundational change that
enables all other Azure integrations.

- New factory.py with lru_cache'd getters for every domain repo
- SQL repositories for submissions, policies, claims, compliance
- InMemoryComplianceRepository to replace bare dicts in compliance.py
- All API modules now import repos from the factory
- seed_data.py uses factory-provided repos (incl. compliance)
- main.py logs storage_mode on startup; seeds only in memory mode
- repositories/__init__.py updated with new exports
